return.speaker: narrator
return.text: She is on her feet this time. The sword is sheathed; the cairn is at her back; the cloak is fastened the way one fastens it for a road, not for a wait. She nods once when she sees you — the nod of someone who has decided ahead of you and is waiting to be asked.

lyra-decision.speaker: lyra
lyra-decision.text: I have done my thinking. The cairn is no place to spend another winter. If you still mean it — the second blade, the honest road — I will walk with you. The price is not gold. The price is that you do not lie to me about the work. I have had enough lies about the work to last me.

choices.text: Walk with me. We start now.
Not yet. I need to think.

recruit.speaker: lyra
recruit.text: Aye. Then it is settled. I will hold the rise this time. I will not call the line back from the bog. I will not call it forward into one either. We march on what we can see.

recruit-done.speaker: lyra
recruit-done.text: One thing. If a name of mine comes up on the road — you tell me before you answer for me. I will not have another sergeant write a word for me without my hand on the page. That is the only condition. Lead, then. I will follow.

decline.speaker: lyra
decline.text: Fair. Think. The cairn is here. So am I. The wind has not changed in a year — it will not change in a day. Find me when the answer is settled.
